S1.savoir: Those who don't know where their money goes will never learn to keep it. Tracking your expenses is the first act of financial freedom.
S1.faire: Record your first expense of the week
S1.maitriserDesc: At least 1 transaction recorded this week

S2.savoir: On average, people underestimate their spending by 30%. Your brain forgets small purchases -- the app does not.
S2.faire: Record expenses in at least 3 different categories

S3.savoir: Knowing your weekly budget is like knowing your car's fuel gauge. Without it, you're driving blind.
S3.faire: Record expenses to see the progress bar

S4.savoir: The end-of-week snapshot: how much you earned, spent, and saved. It's the review that builds awareness.
S4.maitriserDesc: A performance record saved this week

S5.savoir: A budget is not a prison. It's a road -- crossing the lines is dangerous, staying in your lane is comfortable.
S5.faire: Finish the week with a spending rate below 100%

S6.savoir: An impulse purchase costs on average 3x more than you think, because it's money that is no longer working for you.
S6.faire: Use the anti-impulse shield at least once

S7.savoir: The 85% rule: the best money managers only spend 85% of their budget. The remaining 15% is their safety margin.
S7.faire: Finish the week under 85% of the budget

S8.savoir: Discipline is not deprivation. It's choosing what truly matters over what's tempting in the moment.
S8.faire: Record at least 1 transaction per day (5 days minimum)

S9.savoir: Saving is not keeping what's left over. It's setting money aside BEFORE spending. Pay Yourself First.
S9.faire: Finish the week with positive savings

S10.savoir: An emergency fund covers 3 months of expenses. Without one, the slightest unexpected event can bring you to your knees financially.
S10.faire: Create a savings goal with the emergency icon

S11.savoir: Consistency beats intensity. Saving 5,000 FCFA every week is better than 50,000 once a quarter.
S11.faire: Make a contribution to a savings goal

S12.savoir: Q1 review: look at your piggy bank. Every franc in there is proof that you can change your future.
S12.faire: Have a positive balance in the piggy bank

S13.savoir: You can't save what you don't earn. Tracking your income is just as important as tracking your expenses.
S13.faire: Record an income entry (salary or other)
S13.maitriserDesc: At least 1 income recorded this week

S14.savoir: Depending on a single income source is walking a tightrope. Diversification is your safety net.
S14.maitriserDesc: A non-salary income recorded this week

S15.savoir: The income-to-expense ratio is your vital sign. Above 1, you're building. Below, you're destroying.
S15.faire: Have a positive net balance (income > expenses) this week

S16.savoir: Each additional income source reduces your vulnerability by 25%. Two sources are better than one.
S16.faire: Have income from at least 2 different sources this month

S17.savoir: Food is often the top spending category. Monitoring it means watching 30 to 40% of your budget.
S17.faire: View the detail of an expense category

S18.savoir: Fixed expenses (housing, telecom) are the most dangerous: we forget them because they are automatic.
S18.faire: Record at least 2 Fixed-type expenses this week

S19.savoir: The category where you spend the most is where you have the greatest room for improvement.
S19.faire: Reduce spending in your top category compared to the previous week
S19.maitriserDesc: Top category spending down vs previous week

S20.savoir: Zero overspend in a category is a victory. 4 categories under budget = excellent week.
S20.faire: Have at least 4 categories under budget this week

S21.savoir: A goal without an amount or deadline is just a dream. Put a number and a date on it to make it real.
S21.faire: Create a goal with an amount AND a deadline
S21.maitriserDesc: A goal with a deadline exists

S22.savoir: Small regular contributions beat large occasional deposits. Consistency builds the habit.
S22.faire: Make 2 contributions to your goals this week

S23.savoir: Having multiple goals in parallel forces prioritization -- the most valuable financial skill.
S23.faire: Have at least 2 active goals simultaneously

S24.savoir: Q2 review: compare your financial score today with the one from the start. Progress is the real victory.
S24.faire: Have a financial score that has improved since Q1

S25.savoir: EPR (Achievable Potential Savings) is YOUR personalized target. It's not arbitrary -- it's calculated based on YOUR income and expenses.
S25.faire: Have an active EPR target and recorded expenses

S26.savoir: The 67/33 split: two-thirds of your savings for hard savings, one-third for fun. Balance prevents frustration.
S26.faire: Reach a minimum grade of B this week (score >= 7/10)

S27.savoir: The surplus beyond the EPR target is free capital. It's the accelerator for your projects.

S28.savoir: 4 consecutive weeks of positive savings = habit formation. Your brain starts doing it automatically.

S29.savoir: The performance calendar is your financial mirror. Green weeks are your victories, red weeks are your lessons.
S29.faire: View the calendar in the Performance tab

S30.savoir: The trend matters more than any single result. 3 weeks of improvement are worth more than 1 perfect week.
S30.faire: Have an upward trend on the financial score

S31.savoir: Objective vs Actual: the section that shows if your plan is on track. A gap > 20% calls for an adjustment.
S31.faire: Have a prorated execution rate between 80% and 120%

S32.savoir: Grade A is not a luxury. It's achievable if you spend less than 70% of your weekly budget.
S32.faire: Achieve a grade of A or A+ this week

S33.savoir: The end of the month is the classic trap: expenses pile up, the budget melts. Anticipate by spending less at the start of the month.
S33.faire: Finish the week under 80% of the budget

S34.savoir: Every goal achieved proves you have the ability to reach the next one. Financial confidence is built through proof.
S34.faire: Have at least 1 completed savings goal

S35.savoir: A financial score above 70 puts you in the top 20% of money managers. Above 80, in the top 10%.

S36.savoir: Q3 review: 9 months of discipline. Your piggy bank and your goals tell the story of your transformation.
S36.faire: Have a significantly positive cumulative savings balance
S36.maitriserDesc: Net piggy bank > 0 with 20+ weeks of history

S37.savoir: Everyone has bad weeks. A financial master limits the damage and bounces back the following week.
S37.faire: If you overspent last week: get back under budget this week
S37.maitriserDesc: Bounce back after overspend, or week under budget

S38.savoir: An emergency fund is not a goal -- it's an obligation. It should represent 3x your monthly expenses.

S39.savoir: Income consistency is measured over 8 weeks. If you reach 80% of your forecasts, your model is solid.
S39.maitriserDesc: Income recorded in at least 4 of the last 8 weeks

S40.savoir: Financial stress drops by 60% when you have a plan AND follow it. The plan alone is not enough.
S40.faire: REG score >= 80 AND PRE score >= 70 (via saved financial score)

S41.savoir: Year 2 of the plan doubles the EPR target. If you master Year 1, Year 2 is a natural acceleration, not a shock.
S41.faire: Have 10+ weeks with grade B or higher in history

S42.savoir: 3 active savings goals = future diversification. Emergency + Fun + Project = perfect balance.
S42.faire: Have 3 active goals of different categories

S43.savoir: Your 5 financial score levers are like the 5 fingers of your hand. The weakest one determines the strength of your grip.

S44.savoir: Wealth is not built in a day, but it is built every day. Your consistency over the last 11 months proves it.

S45.savoir: The last 3 weeks of the year are the hardest (holidays, social pressure). Staying on track here is the ultimate proof of mastery.
S45.faire: Keep the budget under 90% despite the period

S46.savoir: Every contribution to your goals is a vote for the person you want to become.
S46.faire: Contribute to at least 2 different goals this week

S47.savoir: Financial score >= 75 over 48 weeks. You are among the 15% who finish what they start.

S48.savoir: Annual review: count your achieved goals, your cumulative savings, your score progression. You are no longer the same person as in Week 1.
S48.faire: Have completed at least 40 out of 48 challenges
